rulings.Case Title: PHILIP THOMASCHIMA VS. ESURANCE INCORPORATED, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 2, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PARAGRAPH 11-17 AND PARAGRAPH 34, SENTENCE RELATED TO WRONGFUL DENIAL OF BENEFITS, WHICH ARE IRRELEVANT TO PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION. MOTION GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND REGARDING LOST EARNINGS IF PLAINTIFF CAN FACTUALLY SUPPORT IT. MOTION DENIED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGE ALLEGATIONS AND PREJUDGMENT INTEREST AS PROVIDED BY LAW. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP THOMASCHIMA VS. ESURANCE INCORPORATED, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al

rulings.Case Title: KAO XIONG VS. THE WACKENHUT CORPORATION A FLORIDA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 2, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT THE WACKENHUT CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE WORD "DRAWEE" IN LABOR CODE SECTION 212(c) MEANS "THE BANK ON WHICH THE CHECK IS DRAWN" WESTON V. FED EX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC. JANUARY 27, 2010_F SUPP. 2ND_(2010 WL 1641065) (CITING BLACK'S LAW DICTIONARY 495 (6TH ed. 1990); THE AMERICAN HERITAGE COLLEGE DICTIONARY, 419 (3D EDITION 1997). THEREFORE, SECTION 212(c) DOES APPLY TO THIS CASE BECAUSE THE DRAWEE IS A BANK, SPECIFICALLY BANK OF AMERICA. LABOR CODE SECTION 212(c) REQUIRES THAT, "THE INSTRUMENT SHALL BE NEGOTIABLE AND PAYABLE IN CASH, ON DEMAND, WITHOUT DISCOUNT, AT ANY PLACE OF BUSINESS OF THE DRAWEE CHOSEN BY THE PERSON ENTITLED TO ENFORCE THE INSTRUMENT." THE COURT FINDS THAT "ON DEMAND" MEANS "WHEN PRESENTED OR ON REQUEST FOR PAYMENT." BLACK'S LAW DICTIONARY 8TH ed. 2004.) "DISCOUNT" MEANS "A REDUCTION FROM THE FULL AMOUNT OR VALUE OF SOMETHING." Id. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE CHECKS WERE PAYABLE "ON DEMAND" AND "WITHOUT DISCOUNT," AND TO THE ENTITLEMENT OF RESTITUTIONARY RELIEF. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CHUBB CUSTOM INSURANACE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 2, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT CHUBB CUSTOM INSURANACE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DENIED. THE DUTIES TO INDEMNIFY AND DEFEND ARE DISTINCT FROM AN INSURED'S DUTY TO SETTLE WHICH IS IMPLIED. AN INSURER MAY BE HELD LIABLE FOR JUDGMENT IN EXCESS OF POLICY LIMITS WHERE IT HAS BREACHED AN IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING BY UNREASONABLY REFUSING TO ACCEPT A SETTLEMENT OFFER WITHIN THE POLICY LIMITS. BECAUSE THE DUTY TO SETTLE IS IMPLIED, IT IS NOT POSSIBLE FOR CHUBB TO UTILIZE POLICY LANGUAGE TO LIMIT ITS OBLIGATION TO SETTLE. IF AN INSURED'S FAILURE TO SETTLE WAS TRULY UNREASONABLE AND THE PROPOSED SETTLEMENT IMPLICATED THEIR COVERAGE, IT CANNOT RELY ON THE LACK OF SIR EXHAUSTION TO EXCUSE ITS BAD FAITH CLAIMS. AT ALL TIMES, THE UNDERLYING PLAINTIFFS' SETTLEMENT OFFERS AND THE RESERVE SET BY CHUBB EXCEEDED THE SIR AMOUNT. WHETHER OR NOT THERE WAS A SUBSTANTIAL RISK OF RECOVERY BEYOND THE POLICY LIMITS WITH RESPECT TO THE UNDERLYING ACTION AND WHETHER CHUBBS FAILURE TO SETTLE WAS UNREASONABLE GIVEN THAT PURPORTED RISK ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THE 998 OFFERS WERE IN EXCESS OF CHUBB'S POLICY LIMITS. EVEN IMPUTING CP'S KNOWLEDGE OF THE 998 OFFERS THROUGH AN AGENCY - PRINICIPAL RELATIONSHIP, CHUBB NEVER EXPRESSLY INQUIRED OF CP/CMS/IPHFHA WHETHER THEY WERE WILLING TO CONTRIBUTE THE AMOUNT IN EXCESS OF POLICY LIMITS. A CONFLICT DEVELOPS BETWEEN THE CARRIER AND THE INSURED WHEN AN OFFER TO SETTLE IS MADE IN EXCESS OF POLICY LIMITS AND THE ASSURED IS WILLING AND ABLE TO PAY THE EXCESS. SEE CONTINENTAL CAS. CO. V. UNITED STATES FID. & GUAR. CO. (N.D. CAL 1981) 516 F. SUPP. 384. A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT IS RAISED WHETHER CHUBB PROVIDED INFORMATION TO THE INSUREDS NECESSARY TO PROPERLY AND REASONABLY EVALUATE THE SETTEMENT OFFERS. FFIC MAY STEP INTO CP'S SHOES AND ASSERT ITS RIGHTS WITH RESPECT TO CHUBB'S DUTY TO SETTLE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NANCY D. WOLD VS. PAUL FINANCIAL, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Dismissal Of Pltf'S Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 473
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 2, 2010, line 5, PLAINTIFF NANCY WOLD'S Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Dismissal Of Plaintiff's Complaint Pursuant To CCP 473. GRANTED. CCP SECTION 473(b). FURTHERMORE, THE CLERK'S OFFICE WILL NOT PERMIT THE FILING OF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT UNTIL AN ORDER SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER AND GRANTING LEAVE TO AMEND HAS BEEN FILED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LEO SANDERS JR. VS. DAVID DENG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 2, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS DAVID DENG AND ANGELITA DENG'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PRIOR DEMURRERS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, AND PLAINTIFF'S SUBSEQUENT DISMISSALS WITHOUT PREJUDICE NOT PROPER. SEE RUTTER CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL SECTION 11:19. THE GRAVAMAN OF THE 2 PRIOR ACTIONS AND THIS ACTION IS THE SAME, AND PLAINTIFF IS BARRED FROM BRINGING THIS ACTION BY THE DISPOSITIONS OF ACTIONS ONE AND TWO. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HUNTER LABORAORIES INC., VS. JESSE FIERRO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 2, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT JESSE FIERRO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
